title: Fix 'Enter The Realm' button — dead click handler in CharacterSelectScreen

requirements.text: In CharacterSelectScreen.tsx, audit the 'Enter The Realm' button's onClick handler end-to-end. Identify and fix every reason the button produces zero feedback and does not navigate when clicked. Specifically: (1) confirm the button is not conditionally disabled or rendered without an onClick; (2) confirm the handler calls the onSelectCharacter (or equivalent) prop with the selected character's ID; (3) confirm no outer element is swallowing the click event (e.g. a pointer-events-none overlay or a stopPropagation call); (4) confirm the selected character state is non-null at the time the button is clicked so the ID passed is valid; (5) remove any guard or early-return that silently prevents the callback from firing.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Enter The Realm' with a character highlighted produces an immediate navigation response — no blank screen, no spinner, no silence.
The onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent prop) is invoked with the correct character ID on every click.
The button is never silently disabled or pointer-events-blocked when a character is selected.
No click event is swallowed by a parent element.
After the callback fires, the app transitions to the character sheet / dungeon select screen without requiring an additional user action.

requirements.text: In App.tsx, audit the onSelectCharacter callback (or equivalent) that is passed as a prop to CharacterSelectScreen. Verify: (1) the callback is defined as a stable function (useCallback or equivalent) and is not undefined at render time; (2) it correctly writes the selected character's ID into App-level state (e.g. activeCharacterId); (3) after setting the ID it immediately updates the routing state to transition to the character sheet / dungeon select screen; (4) no intermediate loading flag, isLoading guard, or conditional render silently blocks the screen transition after the callback fires; (5) if any such guard exists, ensure it evaluates to false (i.e. not blocking) by the time the callback completes.
acceptanceCriteria: onSelectCharacter is never undefined when CharacterSelectScreen renders.
Invoking the callback sets activeCharacterId in App state to the correct character ID.
The routing state transitions to the character sheet / dungeon select screen synchronously after the callback fires.
No loading flag or guard condition remains true and blocks the transition.
The transition occurs on the first click without needing a second attempt or page reload.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not alter backend logic or Motoko files for this fix.
The fix must be contained to CharacterSelectScreen.tsx and App.tsx only (plus any directly imported helper used solely by these two files).
nonGoals: Redesigning the character select screen UI.
Changing character data fetching or query logic beyond what is necessary to unblock the callback.
